app.title: AutoHydro Argentina
app.subtitle: Hydrological calculations with verified IDF data
app.tagline: Professional hydrological calculations for civil works in Argentina

calculator.title: Hydrological Calculator
calculator.locationPlaceholder: E.g.: Las Flores creek, Chascomús district
calculator.modified_rational_desc: For medium basins (5–50 km²). Includes areal reduction factor.
calculator.scs_cn_desc: Curve Number based. For any basin size.
calculator.tcInfo: Select one or more formulas. The average will be used for design.
calculator.cnHint: Calculated automatically from land use and soil group
calculator.projectNamePlaceholder: E.g.: Hydraulic design for industrial plant access

results.title: Hydrological Analysis Results
results.comparisonDisclaimer: Values from different methods are indicative. The selected method is recommended for the entered basin characteristics.
results.disclaimer: Notice: These results are estimates for preliminary stages. Verify with updated local studies for final designs.

warnings.prefilled_flow: Design discharge auto-filled from hydrological analysis.
warnings.sf_tr_limit: Return period limited to TR ≤ {{tr}} yr (maximum reliable for this series).
warnings.short_series: Data series shorter than 15 years. Results are indicative only.

sources.pageSubtitle: Full transparency on data sources and calculation methods implemented.

validation.maxOne: Must be a value between 0 and 1
validation.cnCategoriesRequired: Add at least one land use category

errors.calculationFailed: Calculation error. Please verify the entered data.
errors.interpretationFailed: AI interpretation unavailable. The result is still valid.
errors.reportFailed: Error generating PDF. Please try again.
errors.networkError: Connection error. Verify the server is running.

home.heroTitle: Hydrological calculations with verified IDF data
home.heroSubtitle: Calculate design discharges using intensity-duration-frequency data from official Argentine sources.
home.heroDescription: Calculate design discharges using IDF data from official Argentine sources: APA Chaco, INA-CRA, SsRH Neuquén, UTN-ER, UNL, INA-CIRSA, INTA, UNT-FACET. Rational Method, SCS-CN, Tc formulas, Manning and culverts.
home.localitiesSubtitle: 18 localities in 9 provinces. Sources: APA (Chaco/Formosa), INA-CRA (Mendoza/Catamarca), SsRH Neuquén, UTN Concordia, UNL, INA-CIRSA (Córdoba/Salta), INTA (Buenos Aires), UNT-FACET (Tucumán).
home.moreLocalities: New localities will be added as official sources are verified.
home.viewFullDetails: View full details and IDF tables →
home.calculatorsSubtitle: Integrated hydrological and hydraulic calculation tools.
home.methodologyText: AutoHydro implements standard hydrological engineering methods: Rational Method, SCS-CN (USDA), 6 Time of Concentration formulas, Manning equation and culvert sizing according to FHWA HDS-5. IDF data come exclusively from verified official sources.
home.disclaimerText: This tool generates estimates for preliminary design stages. For final designs, always verify with the most recent local hydrological studies.
home.disclaimerFull: This tool generates estimates for preliminary design stages. For final designs, always verify with the most recent local hydrological studies and applicable provincial regulations.
home.toolHydrologyDesc: Design discharges using Rational Method and SCS-CN. Tc analysis, CN and PDF report generation.
home.toolManningDesc: Open channel capacity: trapezoidal, rectangular, circular and triangular.
home.toolCulvertsDesc: Culvert sizing per FHWA HDS-5: inlet and outlet control.
home.toolHyetogramDesc: Design storm with temporal distribution: Alternating Blocks, SCS Type II, Chicago, Uniform.

about.title: About AutoHydro Argentina
about.pageSubtitle: AutoHydro Argentina — open source hydrological calculation tool.
about.developerBio: Civil engineer with experience in hydrology and hydraulics. Worked at the Provincial Water Administration (APA) of Chaco in hydrological studies and urban stormwater drainage design.
about.projectText1: AutoHydro Argentina is an open source hydrological calculation tool for professional practice in Argentina. The included IDF data covers 18 localities in 9 provinces and comes from verified sources: APA (Chaco), UTN-ER / Dirección de Hidráulica (Entre Ríos), UNL-CURIHAM (Santa Fe), IHLLA-CONICET (Buenos Aires), INTA (Buenos Aires), INA-CIRSA (Córdoba and Salta), INA-CRA (Mendoza and Catamarca), SsRH (Neuquén) and UNT-FACET (Tucumán).
about.projectText2: The project incorporates 9 different IDF models to accommodate the methodological diversity of Argentine sources. New localities are added only when data from verifiable technical publications is available: official resolutions, peer-reviewed papers, and technical reports from water authorities.
about.contactText: Found a bug, have a suggestion, or want to collaborate? Open an issue on GitHub or contact me directly on LinkedIn.

footer.description: Hydrological calculation tool with verified IDF data. Open source, free professional use.
footer.contactText: Ing. Ammar Mahfoud — Civil Engineer, Hydrology and Hydraulics. Bugs and suggestions via GitHub or LinkedIn.
